rulings.Case Title: HEZZACK DANIELS VS. RECOLOGY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 4, DEFENDANTs RECOLOGY INC. (FROM CASE# 508886) and RECOLOGY SAN FRANCISCO's (FROM CASE# 508886) DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. The amended complaint does not allege that plaintiffs exhausted their administrative remedy by filing a claim with the Labor Commissioner pursuant to Labor Code § 98.7. See MacDonald v. State of CA (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 67. Plaintiffs fail to show that they can cure that defect by amendment. rulings.Case Title: BRET CORNELL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANTs POLICE OFFICER RICHARD BODISCO, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER DAVID BRANDT, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE OFFICER JESSE FARRELL, INDIVIDUALLY, POLICE SERGEANT WALLACE GIN, INDIVIDUALLY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Granted as to defendants Richard Bodisco and Jesse Farrell and denied as to defendants David Brandt, Wallace Gin and the City and County of San Francisco. As to Bodisco and Farrell, even assuming that they wrongfully detained and/or wrongfully arrested plaintiff Bret Cornell, the undisputed material facts establish that they did not personally use excessive force nor were they in a position, by their intercession or other participation, to have prevented or mitigated the use of excessive force by other officers. As to Brandt, Gin and the City, there are material issues of disputed fact whether they detained Cornell without reasonable suspicion, arrested him without probable cause and, in the course of detaining and/or arresting him, used excessive force. The hearing will be at 9am, not 9:30am. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party (Defendant) is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL BEAL et al VS. ROYAL OAK BAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Statements Of Damages, For Monetary, Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions For Violations Of Discovery Statutes
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 2: Defendants ROYAL OAK BAR'S and ARES PAPAGEORGE'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Statements Of Damages, For Monetary, Issue, Evidentiary, And Terminating Sanctions For Violations Of Discovery Statutes Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFFS HAVE FILED THE REQUESTED STATEMENT OF DAMAGES WHICH MAKES IT UNNECESSARY TO ISSUE AN ORDER FOR THEM TO DO SO. DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR EVIDENTIARY AND TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS DENIED; MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1860.00 AGAINST PLAINTIFFS' ATTORNEYS AS NO JUSTIFICATION WAS PROVIDED AS TO WHY THEY FAILED TO FILE THE REQUESTED STATEMENTS OF DAMAGES PRIOR TO THE FILING OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE AN APPROPRIATE ORDER, SUBMIT TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND PRESENT THE PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE AT THE HEARING ON OCTOBER 8, 2013. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: COUPONS. COM INCORPORATED VS. CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 14, PETITIONER COUPONS. COM INCORPORATED's (Amended) Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award. Petitioner Coupons.com's Petition to Correct and Confirm the Interim Award is denied. The Arbitrator could not have been clearer that he was issuing an interim award and not a partial final award, that there were several issues yet to hear evidence on and decide, and that the JAMS Rules allowed him to proceed in that manner. Pursuant to JAMS Rule 22(i), if an Arbitrator re-opens a hearing, the time limits set forth by the JAMS Rules are extended until the Arbitrator declares the hearing closed. His interpretation of the applicable JAMS Rules is final. Therefore, there is nothing ripe for court confirmation, and there is no basis for the Court to alter the Arbitrator's interim award. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: COUPONS. COM INCORPORATED VS. CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: [Redacted] Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To File Under Seal: 1) Response To Coupons.Com, Inc'S Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award 2) Memo Of Pts & Auth 3) Dec Of James S. Tsuei
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 14, RESPONDENT CATALINA MARKETING CORPORATION's [Redacted] Motion To File Under Seal: 1) Response To Coupons.Com, Inc's Petition To Correct And Confirm Arbitration Award 2) Memorandum Of Points & Authorities 3) Declaration Of James S. Tsuei. Respondents' motion to seal is granted, based on the findings of the Arbitrator. Pursuant to CRC 2.550, the Court finds that (1) there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the record; (2) the overriding interest supports sealing the record; (3) a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; (4) the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and (5) no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. rulings.Case Title: ERIC WARNER VS. SAN FRANCISCO DISTRICT ATTORNEY et al (Provide Access)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 16, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. This Court has no jurisdiction to entertain a civil action collaterally attacking a criminal sentence, let alone a sentence imposed in another county. Even if the Court could address the merits of the case, which it cannot, and even if there were a civil cause of action for breach of a sentencing agreement, which there is not, Plaintiff's claim would fail on the merits for several other reasons. Plaintiff does not allege, let alone present any evidence of, any act by Defendant that breached the agreement. Rather, he alleges that another entity, which is not a party to this case, the San Mateo District Attorney, breached the agreement. Defendant produces sufficient evidence that the statute of limitations has run on any putative breach of contract claim, as the date of the alleged breach occurred on September 7, 2001, and this action was filed in February 2012. (Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, Exhs. B, C; See also CCP §337.) Plaintiff has not produced any evidence supporting his argument that the statute of limitations period has been tolled indefinitely under CCP §352. Plaintiff produces no relevant evidence that he suffered from Post Traumatic Stress Disorder when the alleged breach occurred on September 7, 2001, or that the Post Traumatic Stress order has rendered him insane as contemplated under CCP §352. The record shows that Plaintiff has an adequate remedy at law available to challenge his sentence through a writ of a habeas corpus. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: GENERAL WORKING INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MOTI KAHANA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motiona Nd Motion To Compel Further Responses To Pltf'S 1) Form Interrgoatories-General Set One; 2) Special Interrogatoris, Set One 3) Inspection Demand, Set One. And 4) Requests For Admiissions, Set One, To Deft Todd Daum
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 9: Plaintiffs GENERAL WORKING INC.'S, a Delaware corporation, and GENERAL WORKINGS INC.'S, a Delaware corporation, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's 1) Form Interrgoatories-General Set One; 2) Special Interrogatories, Set One 3) Inspection Demand, Set One. And 4) Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant Todd Daum [Part 1 of 2:] Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: PLAINTIFF GENERAL WORKINGS INC.'S MOTIONS ARE GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART AS FOLLOWS: 1. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES - SET ONE NOS. 2.3,2.5,2.6(B) 12.1,12.2,12.3,12.6,14.2,16.1,17.1,50.2 -50.6 IS GRANTED, IT IS DENIED AS TO NOS. 12.4,12.5&12.7. DEFENDANT TODD DAUM WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION WITHIN 20 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, OR WITHIN 20 DAYS AFTER THE COURT RULES ON HIS PENDING DEMURRER TO THE FAC, UNLESS SUCH RULING IS AN ORDER DISMISSING THE FAC WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND, WHICHEVER DATE IS LATER. 2. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES - SET ONE NOS. 4,11&12 IS GRANTED, IT IS DENIED AS TO NOS. 8&9. DEFENDANT TODD DAUM WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD OUTLINED IN #1 ABOVE. 3. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INSPECTION DEMANDS - SET ONE IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 3,5,10&11 WHEREIN DEFENDANT AGREED TO PRODUCE "RELEVANT DOCUMENTS "SUBJECT O OBJECTIONS, DEFENDANT SHOULD IDENTIFY WITH SPECIFICITY ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS IT IS WITHHOLDING AND THE BASIS FOR SUCH REFUSAL TO PRODUCE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO NOS. 23&24. IT IS DENIED AS TO REQUEST NO. 16 AS IT WAS NOT SET FORTH IN THE SEPARATE STATEMENT IN SUPPORT OF THE MOTION. DEFENDANT TODD DAUM WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBECTION,WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD OUTLINED IN #1 ABOVE. 4. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION - SET ONE NOS. 6,7,8,9,11,12,13,16,17,19&20 IS GRANTED. TO THE EXTENT THAT SUCH RESPONSES ARE OTHER THAN AN UNQUALIFIED ADMISSION, DEFENDANT SHALL RESPONSE IN CODE COMPLIANT LANGUAGE AND PROVIDE IN RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORY 17.1 THE REASONS WHY HE IS UNABLE TO ADMIT AND THE EXTENT OF THE EFFORTS HE MADE TO OBTAIN THE REQUESTED INFORMATION UPON WHICH TO FILE A RESPONSE. DEFENDANT WILL FILE FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN THE TIME PERIOD OUTLINED IN #1 ABOVE. 5. MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HIS COUNSEL IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,500.00. THE AMOUNT REQUESTED BY PLAINTIFF WAS REDUCED BECAUSE THERE COULD HAVE BEEN GREATER COOPERATION IN THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS. [Continued to Part 2 of 2] =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DAVID PIVTORAK VS. DAVID CARL ANDERSON, DBA LAW OFFICE OF DAVID C. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANT DAVID CARL ANDERSON, DBA LAW OFFICE OF DAVID C. ANDERSON's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The Anti-Slapp Motion to Strike under Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. § 425.16 is denied as untimely. (See Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. § 425.16(f).) Defendant had 60 days to file the Anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike after the First Amended Complaint was filed on June 27, 2012. The Anti-SLAPP Motion to Strike was filed on September 10, 2013, well over a year too late. No reason is suggested why the motion could not have been filed on a timely basis. The Court finds no persuasive reason to exercise it discretion to hear an untimely motion under these circumstances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC. VS. CESAR HERNANDEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT CESAR HERNANDEZ' MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES. Denied. Civil Code section 1717.5, which in effect supplies a contractual prevailing party attorney fee clause for cases based on an open book account, is best read in concert with the rules governing attorney fee awards in contract cases. Section 1717(b)(2) states that there is no prevailing party in a contract action that is dismissed or settled before trial, as this action was. Section 1717.5 treats open book account cases as contract cases. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: CATHERINE WESTBROOKS VS. WINE,COM, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT WINE.COM, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. There are triable issues of fact for each cause of action. For the first cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether the incidents on which the hostile work environment claim are based were severe or pervasive or based on more than sporadic, isolated events. For the second cause of action, Plaintiff has presented evidence of retaliation based on protected activity. (See, e.g., AMF no. 12, 36, 37.) For the third and fourth causes of action, Defendant predicates its argument on the arguments raised in the first and second causes of action. For the fifth cause of action, triable issues of fact exist whether the conduct was sufficiently severe and outrageous and whether Plaintiff suffered severe emotional distress. The IIED claim is not clearly barred by the Workers Compensation Exclusivity Rule. (Cabesuela v. Browning-Ferris Industries of California, Inc. (1998) 68 Cal.App.4th 101, 113.) For the sixth cause of action, there are triable issues of fact whether Defendant engaged in wage and hour violations concerning meal and rest breaks and uncompensated overtime. Both parties have violated the rules governing page lengths for summary judgment pleadings. (CRC 3.1113(d).) This is a serious imposition on the Court. These are not "minor" rules that the parties are free to ignore with impunity, and they cannot be evaded by adding pages of argument to other required documents. Counsel are directed to read the rules and to comply with them on all future filings. Failure to do so will cause the Court to weigh exercising its discretion not to consider future oversized pleadings. (CRC 3.1113(g) and 3.1300(d).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN C. MITCHELL VS. BARRELIER ASSOCIATES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses From Pltff John Mitchell
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 5: Defendants DOROTHY KELLER'S and WILLIAM KELLER'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff John Mitchell Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: THIS MOTION IS OFF CALENDAR; PLAINTIFF HAS FILED A DISMISSAL OF THE COMPLAINT. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STAPLETON, DAVID P., THE COURT-APPOINTED RECEIVER VS. 3066 MARKET STREET, LLC et al

rulings.Case Title: CONNIE H. SEBASTIAN VS. SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT SURETY LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to causes of action 1-3. Plaintiff has leave to plead those claims on grounds that are independent from the Unfair Insurance Practices Act (UIPA) (Ins.C. § 790.03). The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as cause of action 4 because plaintiff cannot bring the claim based on the relationship between defendant and its parent corporation, and the relationship between and insurer and insured is not a fiduciary relationship. See Love v. Fire Ins. Exchange (1990) 221 Cal.App. 3d 1136, 1147. The demurrer to cause of action 5 is sustained with leave to amend to allege the terms of the contract with the requisite specificity or by attaching the contract to plead the breach more clearly. The demurrer to the elder abuse cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to plead a claim on some valid basis other than Ins.C. §781 or B&P Code § 17206.1, neither of which can support the claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/PJB)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Resonses To Form Interogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; And For An Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Specified In Pltf'S Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, October 8, 2013, Line 10: Plaintiff RESURGENCE CAPITAL, LLC'S Motion For Order Compelling Resonses To Form Interogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; And For An Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiff's Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: NO OPPOSITION PAPERS HAVE BEEN FILED; ACCORDINGLY THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED AS FOLLOWS: 1. MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, EXCEPT AS TO ATTORNEY/CLIENT PRIVILEGE, TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. SUCH RESPONSES SHALL BE FILED WITHIN 20 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 2. MOTION TO DEEMED THE MATTERS ADMITTED IN PLAINTIFF'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION IS GRANTED, UNLESS DEFENDANT FILES FULL AND COMPLETE RESPONSES WITHIN 20 DAY FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. 3. MONETARY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $260.00 ARE ASSESSED AS AGAINST DEFENDANT AND HER COUNSEL, PAYABLE WITHIN 10 DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT WILL SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER WITH AMENDMENTS CONSISTENT WITH THIS TENTATIVE RULING. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepa@cooley.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. =(302/JPT)
